title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Contract Lens: Signal Drain Exit
summary: The service reads configuration during requests, starts background promises without ownership, mixes module systems, treats signals as ordinary exceptions, and reports ready before dependencies exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts signal drain exit increment in process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.
Sequence configuration, dependency creation, listener binding, liveness, readiness, background ownership, and atomic startup failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Signal Drain Exit · Theory Signal Drain Exit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1718 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay pressure. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.
